summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) to support modern Matrix homeservers (including matrix.org since April 2025) that use Matrix Authentication Service (MAS) instead of password auth.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC auth flow implemented and working end-to-end: browser popup opens, MAS approves, tokens are exchanged, channel connects. Multiple bugs fixed iteratively through live testing. PR #730 created and merged to main.

repo: ChannelPlugin trait methods must be overridden in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin`, not as inherent methods on `impl MatrixPlugin` — gateway dispatches through `dyn ChannelPlugin` and can't see inherent methods
matrix_auth().logged_in() returns false for OIDC sessions; use client.user_id().is_some() to check session state for both auth methods
MAS requires redirect_uri to use http:// (not https://) for loopback addresses per RFC 8252, even though Moltis server runs HTTPS
MAS rejects client_uri pointing to localhost — must use a real HTTPS project URL (https://moltis.org) in ClientMetadata
bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None) is a no-op if cross-signing already exists from a prior session; need reset_identity() when the device lacks signing keys
reset_identity() with OAuth type returns CrossSigningResetAuthType::OAuth requiring browser approval at a MAS URL before reset(None) can complete
The pending identity reset handle is in-memory only — lost on restart, requiring re-triggering reset_identity() on retry
OIDC session persistence uses a background task via client.subscribe_to_session_changes() to save refreshed tokens to disk
RPC response payload is at res.payload not res.result — a common mistake when adding new RPC methods

code.finding: matrix_auth().logged_in() only works for password/token sessions, not OIDC — fixed to client.user_id().is_some()
normalize_redirect_uri() rewrites https://localhost to http://localhost for MAS compliance; client_uri must be project URL not localhost
ChannelPlugin trait needs default noop oidc_start/oidc_complete methods so gateway can dispatch generically via dyn ChannelPlugin
oauth_start was building a hardcoded minimal config ignoring ownership_mode, dm_policy, etc. from the JS form — all form fields must be passed through
Safari doesn't repaint button text nodes when Preact patches them in-place; fix is key=${savingState} on the button element to force DOM recreation

workflow: Safari has a browser paint bug: when Preact patches a text node inside <button> while opacity transition runs on :disabled, old text pixels persist as ghost overlay — fix by keying the button element on state changes
Light-on-light color schemes (text-sky-100/emerald-100 on bg-sky-500/10) are unreadable; use dark-on-light instead (text-sky-900 on bg-sky-50)
Greptile code review caught real bugs across 4 iterations: wrong response field (res.result vs res.payload), blocking I/O in async fns, security token exposure via Debug derive, file permissions
E2E tests that mock the RPC layer can't catch endpoint path mismatches or server policy rejections — need integration tests against real MAS for OAuth flows
The /auth/callback route exists (not /api/oauth/callback) — always verify route paths against actual server registration before coding redirect URIs

friction: Multiple back-and-forth iterations fixing redirect_uri issues: first https vs http, then wrong callback path (/api/oauth/callback vs /auth/callback), then client_uri pointing to localhost

Assistant went in circles on the Safari ghost button issue through 6+ attempts (CSS transitions, span wrapping, key on span, key wrapper div) before finding the correct fix (key on the button element itself)
Cross-signing bootstrap requires browser approval from MAS for identity resets — this UX flow wasn't visible in the UI after Moltis restart because the in-memory handle was lost
Channel list didn't auto-refresh after OIDC complete + ownership setup; user had to reload to see ownership error and retry buttons
Ownership mode checkbox was hidden for OIDC (only shown for password), causing all OIDC accounts to default to user_managed
The duplicate account guard was missing — users could connect the same Matrix user multiple times via OIDC
open_items: OIDC channel restore on Moltis restart may be unreliable — if the stored OIDC session fails to restore, the channel appears in the DB but not in the plugin's accounts map, causing 'unknown channel account' errors on retry_ownership
Cross-signing reset via OIDC requires browser approval and an in-memory handle that is lost on restart — needs persistent handle storage or re-triggering reset_identity() on retry
No E2E tests that exercise the full OIDC flow against a real MAS instance — current Playwright tests only mock the RPC layer
oidc_issuer config field was removed as unused — matrix-sdk 0.16 has no API to override the OIDC issuer URL, but this may be needed for self-hosted MAS with custom issuer URLs in future
Duplicate Matrix account detection only fires on start/complete — no deduplication when Moltis loads channels from store on restart
The 'Retry ownership' button flow for OIDC after Moltis restart needs proper re-triggering of reset_identity() rather than assuming an in-memory handle exists
